reviews.review_text: Drove here from brisbane, arrived half an hour before they would close. Lady at the reception was on the phone with someone and didnt want to check us in because they would close in half an hour. It doesnt take that long to check in… We felt unwelcomed and like the lady just didnt want to go to the trouble of doing her work…
Perfect spot- sites are a bit small and can feel very tight when it is a busy weekend
Lovely place, council prices even to stay in Tent in the main section! Highly overpriced
Right on the beach
Great spot for a holiday in a van have some cabins as well. Right on the beach

We’ve been coming here annually for many years. Our favourite getaway on the coast. Fantastic location, Wayne and Narelle always lovely hosts, recently upgraded cabins are very nice, well appointed. Wifi is a bit dodgy(not a bad thing to switch off sometimes), and park itself is a little tired but well maintained. Beach is patrolled on weekend.

Abusive, threatening & intimidating park manager. We will not be back as much as we liked the area, the abuse from the manager towards my child and myself was un called for and out of line. He chose to abuse us instead of saying who he was and what he wanted calmly. His threats to kick us out and telling myself to stop being a princess because I said he was scaring my child and that there no need to yell. Disgusting how could someone like that be allowed to run a family friendly park. I would like to take this matter further as his unhinged behaviour was in excusable.

We pulled up to try and book a nights accommodation and the old guy ( worker from the park ) was standing outside near the boom gate and was so rude to my wife saying there was no available sites which was fine , the park has an overflow section and when we asked about this he said it was only for tents yet there was already 2 camper vans in there !!
So rude won’t ever try there again
Hidden gem off the beaten track. Don't tell anyone about this beach, it's the last of the over crowded over priced CP in the far north NSW and just 10 minutes across the border.

Beautiful spot, awful customer service. The man at the office was rude, visibly irritated, and made us feel unwelcome for absolutely no reason. My sister had a similar experience a couple of years ago, and other reviews mention the same thing. Such a lovely spot — it would be great if the welcome matched the location.

Wir haben spontan einen sehr schönen Stellplatz für unser Wohnmobil bekommen. Ganz nah am Strandübergang. Toiletten, Duschen und Campküche waren einwandfrei, großzügig und sauber! Aus einer Übernachtung wurden drei und das bei allerbestem Wetter. Vielen Dank an Wayne für den morgendlichen Englischunterricht!
Uns hat es sehr gut gefallen, wir empfehlen den Holidaypark uneingeschränkt weiter!
1 night not possible

The Surfari tent was a bit rough in bad weather and felt damp, but the awesome staff moved us to a lovely cabin. It was clean, cosy and well equipped. Quiet at night and right on the beach. Highly recommended.
Great location live this place and all welcomed by park manager Wayne

What a spot! Park was very busy but very quiet and to our liking. Cook cabin 3 was the place to be. Friends stayed with caravan and we all had a brilliant time.
Walks on beach. Walk to lighthouse. Beers on chairs on sand dunes watching whales do their thing whilst sun setting. Anglers fishing on the beach. Top spot. We shall return!👍

Not very kid friendly and no shade in the camp ground area. Camp ground area is also on a public road. Beach is beautiful though and there is more shade in the caravan park area. There’s not really much of playground and the management and ‘regulars’ don’t seem to like kids being there.

What a fantastic place to stay this was! Loved having such easy access to both beach and bush - got myself a wonderfully restorative nature fix while we were there. Also loved the shady trees in many parts of the caravan park (unlike some other treeless parks around the place). Wayne was great - very friendly and helpful. I’m so keen to get back to this lovely caravan park that we’ve already booked another stay. Cannot wait! Love Fingal!
Beautiful place to stay
Great spot to chill out for a few weeks

After a night of heavy rain where our tent got soaked, we spent the entire day driving up the coast. We arrived at the campground around 5 PM and asked for a powered site, hoping to plug in a small heater to dry the inside of our tent. Unfortunately, only unpowered sites were available. We explained to the old man at reception that we would check if another campground nearby had a powered site so we could dry our gear.

After searching in town with no luck, we came back to the campground at 5:50 PM. Reception was supposed to close at 6 PM. But as we arrived, the old man came out and said, "It's too late, I'm closing." Just like that.

So there we were — three young travelers, left in the dark with nowhere to sleep. We ended up crammed in our car with all our wet belongings, simply because this man wouldn’t take five minutes to help us check in, even though we were still within the posted hours. A very disappointing experience, especially after a long and difficult day.

After a night of heavy rain where our tent got soaked, we spent the entire day driving up the coast. We arrived at the campground around 5 PM and asked for a powered site, hoping to plug in a small heater to dry the inside of our tent. Unfortunately, only unpowered sites were available. We explained to the man at reception that we would check if another campground nearby had a powered site so we could dry our gear.

After searching the town with no luck, we came back to the campground at 5:50 PM. Reception was supposed to close at 6 PM. But as we arrived, the man came out and said, "It's too late, I'm closing." Just like that.

So there we were — three young travelers, left in the dark with nowhere to sleep. We ended up crammed in our car with all our wet belongings, simply because this man wouldn’t take five minutes to help us check in, even though we were still within the posted hours. A very disappointing experience, especially after a long and difficult day.
Très mauvais personnel
Gave this mob a call to try and book a site and each time I was told to leave a message it automatically hung up in stead. Not very good service if you ask me. Won’t be going back there.

This is our 4th time here and we can not fault it. The view from cabin 2 is faultless, walk to beach is 2 mins. The cabin was spotless, all the amenities you would want and need. The bed in the master bedroom is so comfortable, in fact its more comfortable than 80% of the 5 star hotels we have stayed in. I have already booked another 2 trips here for the coming months.
Right on the safest beach on the Coast! Fingal Head
Such friendly staff here and the epic location!

Awesome caravan park. Outstanding cleanliness in facilities. Plenty of room and the beach is superb.
10/10
Right on beach. Plenty of bird life.

Had booked for 3 nights and planned to drive down from Brisbane after work (28/3) so not arriving until the evening. Impending rain of over 100mm forecast for the weekend so notified Tuesday evening (25/3) that we don't want to camp in the rain so looked to move dates. Whoever is behind the email there states no refund as we are within 72 hours, even without a confirmed arrival time on-site.  Unfriendly and unhelpful.

A great place for a beach holiday with your family. The facilities are great and there are plenty of cabins, powered and unpowered spots available. There are so many activities near by, like snorkeling with turtles, paddle boarding, and tropical fruit world!
Very nice  spot...

Excellent.  One of the best places I’ve ever camped.  Amazing and quiet.  Beautiful.  The parks management was very professional and friendly.  I had some special needs and management went out of there way too ensure my comfort.  As good as it gets.

What a disappointment. Unpowered camp sites are located in a swamp paddock outside of the holiday park. No facilities close by. When passing on my feedback to Wayne the manager upon leaving, (who was quick to shut me down and could barely even make eye contact with me) he told me I should have done my homework and looked at the map online where I would be camping. Although strange it doesn't list the swamp that you're putting your tent up next to. Admitted in his own words that 'they are not worth 70 dollars a night for a site full of midgees by the swamp I wouldn't book them either but if I advertised them as that then we wouldn't get any bookings on them.'

Love this park been going here for years unfortunately our last stay we had an issue whilst in the park with damage to our car there was a camera in the park facing the car called the office to be told the park cameras don't work  not the answer I was hoping for due to this I can't bring myself back to booking a holiday here I do hope they get the cameras sorted as it is a necessity in the times we live in
We were there in November. Beach and surf was beautiful. Amenities were very good. Not a lot of room between Van sites

Recently spent a few nights here. Was rather underwhelmed. The location is great, on the beach, and it's relatively quiet, amenities were kept mostly clean.* Apart from that there's not a lot to rave about.

The kids playground is non existent, all that's left is 2 swings and the baby swing safety catch is ceased shut so none operational.

Could tell lawns had not been cut in a few weeks.

Apart from the 'private' sites with permanent residents there are also a heap of what seem to be long term/semi permanent vans in place and their sites are not well kept, long grass growing through everything etc.

Two unserviced sand and cigarette filled planters greet you at the toilets for the smokers, (even though rules state no smoking in common areas.)

We got 'scolded' by a male staff member when our toddlers were on their scooters in front of our van burning off energy at dusk, my wife and I standing with them (no scooters and bikes at night, no problem, but it wasn't dark yet). Would have minded less if he was more polite about it.

*There is an out of service shower, I happened to catch a glimpse of the inside and it wasn't pleasant, clearly no sign of getting fixed. Irrelevant, keeping it clean shouldn't be a hard ask. Mind you that whole row of showers had little to no water pressure.

Booms in and out of the park were not functional either.
had a camp site with beach views. beautiful spot and great customer service
Great spot, the beach is awesome.
Beautiful spot right next to the beach
Great place to stay. Facilities clean and all staff very friendly. Great beach and river walks

Love this spot and caravan park. Nice and quiet but still close if you forgot anything. Shady areas, good bathroom and laundry facilities, very basic kitchen set up but all well kept. Already planning on coming back.

Lovely little park, nice & quiet, friendly staff||This is not a mark against the park as it’s not something they can control but the 5G mobile data is absolutely horrendous in the park ( we were using Telstra ) getting only 1 bar reception and getting regulars SOS messages appearing on our phones. Upon leaving and only 3 minutes up the road in a car 4 bar reception & data access is avail but that doesn’t help inside the park. ||Although this may not affect some people but for people who are travelling working online, or people who have teenagers or kids who could use distraction on rainy days then you need to be aware the mobile data is the non existent! ||For us personally if we had of known of the above we prob wouldn’t have booked in this park 😊

Lovely little park, nice & quiet, friendly staff
This is not a mark against the park as it’s not something they can control but the 5G mobile data is absolutely horrendous in the park ( we were using Telstra ) getting only 1 bar reception and getting regulars SOS messages appearing on our phones. Upon leaving and only 3 minutes up the road 4 bar reception & data access but that doesn’t help inside the park.

Although this may not affect some people for people who are travelling working online, or people who have teenagers or kids who could use distraction on rainy days then you need to be aware the mobile data is non existent!

For us personally if we had of known of the above we prob wouldn’t have booked in this park 😊

Great place to stay.
On the beach ⛱️
My favourite place to relax.

Very relaxed park on the beach. One of our favourite spots so far for a lovely sunrise in the morning and cafe at entrance.

Wayne was very helpful and kind.
Would definitely return.
Great quieter out of the way park with super friendly staff and great surf beach ⛱️ next to the best little cafe on the coast. Will be back .

Short stay beautiful view easy access to the
beach. Very relaxing 😌☺️😎

Great location, family friendly. Accommodation is expensive and lacking. Bushland is overgrown and now almost completely blocks your view. The council needs to trim it back regularly. WiFi sucks big time even though we were close to the park reception. In fact, WiFi is pointless. Phone reception is poor as well. Staff and management are excellent and helpful. Unfortunately, as this is a Tweed Council park, the management are limited in what they can do. Tweed Council, pull your socks up and spend some money on the infrastructure. Considering if you want to book in advance, the Council wants the full amount to hold your booking.

Very disappointed with the price paid and what we got. We paid $38 per night.

We booked an unpowered site and turns out to be outside of the caravan park. The unpowered area is located in a grass esplanade where you won’t have any bins within the esplanade, any light at night and no security as the area is completely open (no code required to access it).

To access the bathrooms and BBQ area (very limited, basically you have the same amenities you would find in a public area), you have to get inside the caravan park.

So my recommendation would be to save your money and go somewhere else as this place is not worth almost $40 a night.
Nicely maintained facilities. Staff was helpful. Close to boat ramps. A nice fish and chip shop down the road. Plenty of fishing spots
Just like to give a shout to Tammy and Kelly in the office. Thanks ladies for looking after our booking.

Can’t beat the beach location. Perfect if you want to hear the waves crashing at night! Thoroughly enjoyed our stay over Easter!
Not heaps to do in the actual park but I guess that’s why people come here.
Kids park had a few swings but nothing else.
Staff were super friendly and helpful.
Bathrooms were very busy with soap often run out but prob hard to keep up in the busy season. Perhaps some mops for showers would be helpful due to the sandy location.
Camp kitchen clean and easy to use!
Laundry and clothes lines were great!
Simple but great park!
Awesome family getaway holiday recommend
Another great stay,even if the high winds did try to spoil it for us.Great location with access to a very good beach.

No complaints about procedures and customer service.

Booked an unpowered site for $38 and it is basically a paddock with a few taps. Totally separate from the rest of the park, camp kitchen and ammenities , it is nice and tidy but a dissapointment
Beautiful location

Great location. Very friendly park staff. Good facilities.
Could use an ice machine or the sale of ice. No major though. Just be more prepared next time we go
called multiple times with no answer at all
Great holiday spot, very well priced, we stayed in a cabin was very clean and right on the beach. Worth a visit

Such a beautiful, quiet location. The beach is gorgeous with a great little headland walk. The park is immaculate with good sized sites and plenty of shade. There is amazing wildlife including nesting curlews and so many dolphins spotted. I just love it here. I highly recommend taking a stroll up to the Sheoak Cafe for a drink on a Saturday afternoon.

Ottimo camping di fronte a una spiaggia stupenda purtroppo i due giorni in cui abbiamo soggiornato tirava un vento fortissimo e non abbiamo potuto godere della spiaggia per rilassarsi ma abbiamo assistito a grandi evoluzioni con kitesurf, inoltre nel campeggio nidificavano i Bush stone curlew ho trovato tutto stupendo lo consiglio !!

Clean place. Nice to be away from the busy towns and good as right on the beach front with a nice lighthouse lookout point nearby.

Has a new council playground next door which is a good one.

Northern toilet block is newer than the southern one, was busy waiting for showers.

Lost a star due to being small sites. Felt very cramped in when full.
Web booking is impossible to use on an android phone. Can't select dates for our stay
Nope. Wayne was very rude on the phone I'd recommend you go elsewhere. Would not even bother calling Fingal head at all.
Great and tidy park, facilities very clean and well maintained. Staff were friendly.
We enjoyed our short stay at Fingal Head. Amenities are clean and comfortable. If you in the tent site area, I must warn you about the sandflies. They are swarming and awfully painful bites.

Our site was alongside the beautiful beach at Fingal Head,facilities were excellent and the staff were very friendly,we will definitively be going back,a big thank you to Wayne and the staff for making the stay very pleasant.Ideal for families with the beach along side.

Staff were friendly on arrival and made us feel welcome. Holiday Park was generally clean and well maintained, right on the waterfront with direct access to the beach. No local shops, so make sure you have enough supplies, as it's a 4-5km trip each way to Kingscliff.
Excellent.  Friendly on arrival.  Great sites and fresh clean amenities.  Also great camp kitchens.  This is a very clean operation.
Very clean amenities and the park is so peaceful.  We had a beachfront site which was choice!
Great place to visit

Male staff member exceedingly rude on numerous occasions. No cooking facilities apart from bbq & sink. When I asked if there was a kettle to boil water for a cup of tea I was told you need to bring your own & the hot water was hot enough. I was yelled at & had a rude note stuck to my car for parking somewhere that wasn’t signed otherwise. On check-in I asked for a quiet unpowered site & was positioned next to a public road. Such a shame for an amazing location.

Perfect location a stones throw to the beach.  Fingal is a quiet place and this is a simple, quiet park that ticks all the boxes without being fancy.  The Fingal store has all the basics including alcohol and the cafe has good coffee. Would definitely recommend if you want to relax and rest.
Definitely worth a visit. Just gorgeous
You just have to love it,,been going here for over 40yrs on and off👍 thanks Wayne and all for keeping a gem of a place as it should be 🙏👍

What a beautiful park, the sites were big enough. Short walk to the beach, crystal clear water lots of fish around if you like beach fishing.
Would stay again for sure.
